title: Networking Essentials
source: Network Essentials Practice Exam + Question Set

questions.question: What is the compressed format of FE80:030C:0000:0000:0000:ACAD:0000:0040?
What is the preferred format for 2001::1?
What is the binary conversion of 728?
What is the hexadecimal conversion of 1254?
How many usable addresses are in 255.255.255.240?
How many total addresses are in 255.255.255.254?
What is the hexadecimal conversion of 753?
What is the decimal conversion of 1101001100?
What is the hexadecimal conversion of 11100010010?
What is the amount of time, including delays, for data to travel one point to another?
Which IEEE Standard governs the WLAN environment?
When a new standard is proposed, what document is used at each stage of development and in the approval process?
Which of the following is NOT a criterion for choosing network media?

options.text: What is the cost of the media installation?
What is the environment in which the media will be installed?
What is the amount of time it takes to install the media?
What is the amount of data and at what speed must it be transmitted?

questions.question: __________ contains a list of all active ports and the host MAC addresses that are assigned to the LAN.
What does an end device need to communicate on the network? (Choose 3)
Which transmission type refers to one device sending a message to another device in one-to-one communication?
True or False. Routers forward broadcast traffic out of all ports, except the incoming port.
How many bits are in an IPv6 address?
Which step in the DORA process does the DHCP server confirm that the address lease has been accepted?
__________ is the process used to convert private addresses to internet-routable addresses?
True or False. A logical address is used for NIC-to-NIC communication on an ethernet network.
Routers make forwarding decisions based on which address?
True or False. UDP is best effort delivery that does not require acknowledgement of receipt?
What is the port number for the DNS protocol?
Match these troubleshooting utilities to their definitions.

matchPairs.right: Displays the route taken to the destination.
Directly queries the name server for information on the destination domain.
Tests connections to another IP host.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the four basic characteristics that network architects must address to meet user expectations?
True or False. The hexadecimal numbering system has a base 16 numbering system.
Which two layers of the OSI model do the Ethernet standards specify protocols? (Choose two)
Which layer of the OSI model provides services to allow end devices to exchange data across networks?
Which is NOT a troubleshooting method?
The IOS image file is stored in ________?
True or False. The internet is owned by the Institution of Electrical and Electronic Engineers.
Which is not a type of personal data?
__________ is the capacity of a medium to carry data?
A network where two computers are communicating with each other as both a client and server is known as a:
Which wireless technology uses a device-pairing process to communicate and operate over short distances?
What does the acronym SSID stand for?
From this IP address 192.168.5.0/24 what is the Network Portion and what is the host portion?
__________ is a set of rules that determines how something must be done.
RFCs for internet standards are published and managed by what organization?
Which is NOT a Twisted-Pair Cable type?
The process of placing one message format (the letter) inside another message format (the envelope) is called ________?
The command to encrypt all plain-text passwords?
True or False. The IP address of a host machine is the physical address.
True or False. The MAC address can be automatically assigned with the use of a DHCP server.
True or False. Routers are layer 2 devices.
__________ management allows us to remotely connect to devices to perform administrative task.
What two are responsible for transmission at the Network Layer?
Match the type of malware with the definition: spyware.

options.text: gather personal information about a user without permission or knowledge
allow personalization for visited web sites
advertising windows that display when visiting a web site

questions.question: Match the type of malware with the definition: tracking cookies.

options.text: gather personal information about a user without permission or knowledge
a program that demands a ransom to decrypt files
allow personalization for visited web sites
advertising windows that display when visiting a web site

questions.question: Match the type of malware with the definition: pop-ups.

options.text: advertising windows that display when visiting a web site
a self-replicating program that spreads over a network
an application that changes a browser's home page and search engine
gather personal information about a user without permission or knowledge

questions.question: An intruder tries a large number of possibilities in rapid succession to guess a password. As a result, other users on the network are locked out. What type of attack has occurred?
Which three attacks exploit human behavior? (Choose three)
What is the effect of setting the security mode to WEP on a small wireless router?

options.text: It provides the strongest current encryption available for wireless networks.
It encrypts data between the wireless client and the access point.
It disables the broadcast of the SSID, hiding the network from view.
It prevents unauthorized MAC addresses from connecting to the network.

questions.question: Which solution allows external users to access only an internal FTP server on a private network?
What is an inherent security vulnerability of SSID broadcasting?

options.text: It allows a brute force attack against the pre-shared key.
It broadcasts the identity of the network.
It prevents the use of MAC address filtering.
It causes all data transmitted to be unencrypted.

questions.question: A newly purchased client laptop is connecting to a network using a wireless router providing dynamic addressing. Which IP address does the laptop use as a destination address when requesting a dynamically assigned address?

options.text: The router's configured IP address (Default Gateway)

questions.question: A DHCP server uses an address pool of 192.168.10.0/24. If 3 printers on this network need reserved static IP addresses from the pool, how many IP addresses in the pool are left to be assigned to other hosts?
Match each DHCP message type with its description.

matchPairs.right: a client initiating a message to find a DHCP server
A DHCP server responding to the initial request by a client
the client accepting the IP address provided by the DHCP server
the DHCP server confirming that the address lease has been accepted

questions.question: A client device on an Ethernet segment needs an IP address. A DHCP server with IP address 192.168.1.1 is configured and enabled. How will a client device obtain a usable IP address?

options.text: Send a DHCPOFFER message to IP address 255.255.255.255.
Send a DHCPREQUEST message directly to 192.168.1.1.
Send a DHCPDISCOVER message to IP address 255.255.255.255.
Send an ARP request to determine the IP address of the DHCP server.

questions.question: Which two criteria are used to help select a network medium for a network? (Choose two.)

options.text: the distance the selected medium can successfully carry a signal
the color-coding scheme used on the patch cables
the brand name of the network interface card (NIC)
the environment where the selected medium is to be installed

questions.question: What is an advantage of using standards to develop and implement protocols?

options.text: It prevents technology in one layer from affecting other layers.
It requires manufacturers to use the least expensive components.
It speeds up the process of protocol design and implementation.
Products from different manufacturers can interoperate successfully.

questions.question: At which layer of the OSI model would a logical address be added during encapsulation?
Which wireless RF band do IEEE 802.11b/g devices use?
Refer to the exhibit. Which router port connects to the modem provided by the service provider? (Assume port C is the WAN port)
Which type of device filtering can be enabled on some wireless access points or wireless routers?
Using a systematic troubleshooting approach, a help desk technician suspects a problem at Layer 3 of the OSI model. Which two questions are associated with Layer 3? (Choose two.)

options.text: Is the PC configured for DHCP?
From the PC, is the default gateway reachable using the ping command?
Are the cables functional and properly plugged in?
Is the link light active on the network adapter?

questions.question: A customer calls the help line to report a computer problem, but the technician cannot identify the root cause. What should the technician do next to help the customer?

options.text: Disconnect the call and wait for the customer to call back at a later time.
Search the internet for possible solutions while the customer waits on the line.
Tell the customer that a ticket is created and another technician will contact the user soon.
Suggest the customer reboot the computer one more time and call back.

questions.question: After moving offices, a few workstations cannot get a valid IP address. What should be checked first to identify the root cause of the problem?

options.text: Disable and re-enable the network adapter on the workstation.
Make sure the cables are functional and properly plugged.
Verify the Default Gateway IP address on the workstations.
Check the DHCP server logs for address pool exhaustion.

questions.question: A user is setting up a home wireless network. Which type of device must the user have in order to establish the wireless network and provide access to the internet for multiple home devices?
A consumer places a smartphone close to a pay terminal at a store and the shopping charge is successfully paid. Which type of wireless technology was used?
When troubleshooting network problems, where would a network administrator find the configuration information, such as the computer names and the IP addresses that are used?
Which network migration technique encapsulates IPv6 packets inside IPv4 packets to carry them over IPv4 network infrastructures?
A small office wireless router provides internet access. In which two scenarios does the wireless router perform Network Address Translation (NAT)? (Choose two.)

options.text: when a host is sending packets to a remote site owned by the manufacturer of the wireless router in order to request a digital copy of the device manual
when a host is sending packets to a local printer on the same internal network
when the router is receiving a DHCP lease renewal from the ISP
when a host is sending packets to the ISP in order to request a speed increase for Internet services

questions.question: What are two common media used in networks? (Choose two.)
Which two types of interference may affect the data throughput on UTP cables? (Choose two.)
What are two wiring schemes defined by the TIA/EIA organization for Ethernet installation in homes and businesses? (Choose two.)
A host needs to reach another host on a remote network, but the ARP cache has no mapping entries. To what destination address will the host send an ARP request?
Which type of address does a switch use to build the MAC address table?
What are two benefits of using a layered network model? (Choose two.)

options.text: It eliminates the need for any standardization organizations.
It prevents technology in one layer from affecting other layers.
It automatically translates between different protocols.

questions.question: Which two devices are considered end devices? (Choose two.)
Which factor does not influence throughput?

options.text: the load on intermediary network devices
the operating system that is used by end devices
latency and jitter across the network path

questions.question: What is a characteristic of a peer-to-peer application?

options.text: The entire application runs on a single powerful central server.
A dedicated server provides resources that all clients can access.
The primary communication uses the DHCP and DNS protocols.
Each device using the application provides a user interface and runs a background service.

questions.question: Using default settings, what is the next step in the switch boot sequence after the IOS loads from flash?

options.text: Enter ROMMON mode and await administrative command input.
Load the running-config file from RAM.
Check for a boot image on the TFTP server.
Locate and load the startup-config file from NVRAM.

questions.question: Which two files are copied into RAM as a router with the default configuration register setting boots up? (Choose two.)
What are two features of protocols used within the TCP/IP protocol stack? (Choose two.)

options.text: UDP uses a three-way handshake to establish a reliable connection.
TCP automatically includes the MAC address in the segment header.
UDP is used when an application must be delivered as quickly as possible and some loss of data can be tolerated.
TCP mechanisms retransmit data when an acknowledgment is not received from the destination system within a set period of time.

questions.question: A student is uploading files from a phone to a server on another network. Which layer of the TCP/IP model is responsible for providing an addressing scheme to transmit the data between the devices?
Which statement correctly describes data transmission at the transport layer?

options.text: Only UDP handles segmentation and reassembly of data.
TCP is connectionless, while UDP is connection-oriented.
Both UDP and TCP use port numbers.
Port numbers are added at the Internet layer, not the transport layer.

questions.question: What type of technology converts analog voice signals into digital data?
Match the protocol with the function: DNS.

options.text: automatically configures hosts with IP addresses
resolves internet names to IP addresses
transfers files between a client and a server

questions.question: Match the protocol with the function: DHCP.

options.text: ensures reliable, ordered delivery of data
resolves internet names to IP addresses
automatically configures hosts with IP addresses

questions.question: Match the protocol with the function: SSH.

options.text: synchronizes clocks across a computer network
automatically configures hosts with IP addresses
transfers data from a web server to a client

questions.question: Which three protocols should be allowed to provide for the use of email on a network? (Choose three.)
In Software Defined Network (SDN) architecture, what function is removed from network devices and performed by an SDN controller?
A user is configuring a wireless access point and wants to prevent any neighbors from discovering the network. What action does the user need to take?

options.text: Change the default IP address of the router.

questions.question: A data center has updated a physical server to host multiple operating systems on a single CPU, providing each customer with a separate web server without having to allocate a discrete server. What is the networking trend being implemented?
A network administrator installs a network device that focuses on interconnecting independent local networks. At which layer of the hierarchical network design model does this technology reside?
A computer has to send a packet to a destination host in the same LAN. How will the packet be sent?

options.text: The packet will be sent to the default gateway, which will then forward
The packet will be sent directly to the destination host.
The packet will be encapsulated inside an IPv6 packet before being sent.
The packet will be sent as a Layer 3 broadcast to reach the destination.

questions.question: If the default gateway is configured incorrectly on the host, what is the impact on communications?

options.text: The host receives duplicate IP addresses from the DHCP server.
The host can communicate with other hosts on the local network, but is unable to communicate with hosts on remote networks.
The host is unable to communicate with any other hosts, even on the local network.
The host can communicate with remote networks, but its packets will always be filtered by the router.

questions.question: Which three commands are used to set up secure access to a router through a connection to the console interface? (Choose three.)
A network administrator establishes a connection to a switch via SSH. What characteristic uniquely describes the SSH connection?

options.text: allows a configuration file to be transferred from a TFTP server
remote access to a switch where data is sent as plaintext
remote access to a switch where data is encrypted during the session
direct access via the console port using a rollover cable

questions.question: Which type of access is secured on a Cisco router or switch with the enable secret command?

When configuring SSH on a router to implement secure network management, a network engineer has issued the login local and transport input ssh line vty commands. What three additional configuration actions have to be performed to complete the SSH configuration? (Choose three.)

options.text: Create a valid local username and password database.
Configure the correct IP domain name.
Set the enable password to the strongest possible hash.

questions.question: Match the Cisco command to the correct action: Router(config-line)# password Cisco.

options.text: encrypts all plaintext passwords in the configuration
displays a message when the router is accessed
configures a name on the router

questions.question: Match the Cisco command to the correct action: Router(config)# banner motd #.

options.text: configures a password for privileged EXEC mode
displays a message when the router is accessed
configures a name on the router

questions.question: Match the Cisco command to the correct action: Router(config)# hostname CL1.

options.text: allows the administrator to access user EXEC mode
configures a name on the router
displays a message when the router is accessed

questions.question: What is the purpose of the subnet mask in conjunction with an IP address?

options.text: to ensure the IP address is unique across the entire network
to determine the subnet to which the host belongs
to identify the Layer 2 physical address of the host
to specify the default gateway for remote communication

questions.question: Which two statements describe characteristics of network addresses? (Choose two.)

options.text: A MAC address is also referred to as a physical address because it is permanently embedded on the NIC.
An IP address is permanent and does not change when the host moves to a different network.
A valid public IPv4 or IPv6 address is needed in order for devices to communicate over the internet.
A MAC address is a Layer 3 address, while an IP address is a Layer 2 address.

questions.question: What are three characteristics of multicast transmission? (Choose three.)

options.text: A single packet can be sent to a group of hosts.
The destination MAC address is always FF-FF-FF-FF-FF-FF.
Multicast transmission can be used by routers to exchange routing information.
The range of 224.0.0.0 to 224.0.0.255 is reserved to reach multicast groups on a local network.

questions.question: Match the router prompt to the configuration task: RouterA#.

options.text: add a password required to access the privileged EXEC mode

questions.question: Match the router prompt to the configuration task: RouterA(config-if)#.

options.text: add a password required to access the privileged EXEC mode

questions.question: Match the router prompt to the configuration task: RouterA(config-line)#.

options.text: add a password required to access the privileged EXEC mode

questions.question: A network administrator issues the command Switch(config)# interface FastEthernet 0/1 on a Cisco switch. Which term is used to describe the 0/1 part in the command?
What three facts can be determined from the viewable output of the show ip interface brief command? (Choose three.)

options.text: The switch can be remotely managed.
One device is attached to a physical interface.
The default SVI has been configured.
The router has an active routing table.

questions.question: Which command can an administrator execute to determine what interface a router will use to reach remote networks?
What is the delay in the amount of time for data to travel between two points on a network?
What is the primary purpose of a Demilitarized Zone (DMZ) in a network topology?

options.text: To enforce a single security policy across the entire network, both internal and external.
To serve as the primary routing table for all internal traffic.
It isolates external hosts' access to specific company servers while protecting the internal network.
To completely block all traffic from external hosts to internal servers.

questions.question: Convert the following Decimal number into hexadecimal: 941
What is the effect of setting the security mode to Wired Equivalent Privacy (WEP) on a small wireless router?

options.text: It blocks unauthorized devices from joining the network using MAC filtering.
It enables two-factor authentication for all users.
It encrypts data between the wireless client and the access point.
It requires the use of the 802.1X protocol for authentication.

questions.question: Which two actions improve security on a wireless network, even though one is considered obsolete and the other is easily bypassed? (Choose two.)

options.text: Disable the firewall on the router.
Use the default SSID and password.

questions.question: Convert the following IPv6 address into the most compressed form: 0000:0000:8CEA:00DC:DF23:A320:0000:0D00. (Must be in CAPS)
You have a network with a custom subnet mask of 255.255.255.192. How many usable IP addresses are available within each subnet?
Which two criteria are most critical when selecting a network medium for a specific installation? (Choose two.)

options.text: The distance the selected medium can successfully carry a signal
The environment where the selected medium is to be installed
The age of the routers and switches in the facility.
The price of the network interface cards (NICs).

questions.question: Convert the following IPv6 address into the most compressed form: 0000:0000:0000:000D:3440:00BD:CADE:55C0. (Must be in CAPS)
What is the primary means for mitigating virus and Trojan horse attacks on endpoints?
Which two statements are correct about the addresses in the IPv4 and IPv6 protocols? (Choose two.)

options.text: IPv6 addresses are represented by a hexadecimal number.
IPv4 addresses are 64 bits in length.
IPv4 addresses are 32 bits in length.
IPv4 addresses are represented by a hexadecimal number.

questions.question: You have a prefix length of /17. What is your custom subnet mask for an IPv4 address?
What is the broadcast address for the network 192.168.15.220/30?
Which two ports can be used for the initial, out-of-band configuration of a Cisco router or switch? (Choose two.)
An IPv6 address can have a prefix length that ranges from 0 to 128.
Which type of cable connection would typically be used to connect a FastEthernet port on a PC to a switch port?
What feature can be used by an administrator to prevent unauthorized users from connecting to a wireless access point by restricting access to a list of allowed hardware identifiers?
What is the term used when a malicious party sends a fraudulent email disguised as being from a legitimate, trusted source to trick a victim into revealing sensitive information?
A network administrator for a small campus network has issued the show ip interface brief command on a Layer 3 switch. What is the administrator primarily verifying with this command?

options.text: The operational status of all physical access ports and their link light colors.
The entire MAC address table for all connected devices.
The status of the switch interfaces and the IP address configured on the management interface (VLAN 1).
The current spanning-tree topology and root bridge election.

questions.question: IPv6 addresses that are used for communicating only on the same LAN or between a limited number of sites, are known as what type of address?
A network administrator is designing the layout of a new wireless network. Which three areas of concern should be accounted for when building a wireless network? (Choose three.)
What is the result when the command transport input ssh is entered on the switch VTY (Virtual Teletype) lines?

options.text: It allows remote login to the switch only via the Telnet protocol.
It configures the switch to route SSH packets to the default gateway.
Communication between the switch and remote users is encrypted.
It enables Power over Ethernet (PoE) on all VTY interfaces.

questions.question: What are three types of communication methods used in IPv6?
What is the primary software required for a technician to install a hypervisor on a client machine?
What is required for a network administrator to perform out-of-band management tasks on a Cisco device?

options.text: A computer directly connected to the console port.
A computer connected to a network port with a Telnet client.
A Layer 3 switch configured with a static route to the device.
A wireless connection to the device's management SSID.

questions.question: What is the Subnet ID (Network Address) for the host address 192.168.15.145 with a subnet mask of 255.255.255.240?
Convert the following hexadecimal number 0XBAD into its decimal equivalent.
Which two are common causes of a Physical Layer network connectivity problem? (Choose two.)

options.text: An Ethernet cable plugged into a wrong port (e.g., a WAN port instead of a LAN port).
A firewall blocking a specific application port.
A faulty Ethernet cable (e.g., a broken connector or cut wire).
An incorrect IP address configuration on a host.

questions.question: A student is uploading files from a phone to a server on another network. Which layer of the TCP/IP model is responsible for providing an addressing scheme to transmit the data between the different devices and networks?
What is an inherent security vulnerability of Service Set Identifier (SSID) broadcasting?

options.text: It forces the use of the weak WEP encryption protocol.
It prevents the implementation of MAC address filtering.
It disables the router's internal firewall.
It broadcasts the identity of the network.

questions.question: Which interface is the default Switched Virtual Interface (SVI) on a Cisco switch?
A network administrator needs to keep the user name, password, and session contents private when establishing remote CLI connectivity with a switch to manage it. Which access method should be chosen?

A network administrator notices that some newly installed Ethernet cabling is carrying corrupt and distorted data signals. The new cabling was installed in the ceiling close to fluorescent lights and electrical equipment. Which two factors may interfere with the copper cabling and result in signal distortion and data corruption? (Choose two.)
Convert the following hexadecimal number 0XA2F into its decimal equivalent.
Which type of wireless communication is based on 802.11 standards?
Convert the following binary notation 1010101 into its decimal equivalent.
You have a network with a custom subnet mask of 255.255.255.224. How many usable IP addresses are available within each subnet?

Which three commands are used to set up secure access to a router through a connection to the console interface? (Choose three)

options.text: interface con 0, enable secret cisco, login
console password cisco, enable secret, line con 0

questions.question: You have a prefix length of /19. What is your custom subnet mask?

What is indicated by the term throughput?

options.text: The number of packets dropped due to errors during transmission.
The delay experienced by a packet traveling from source to destination.
The maximum theoretical speed of the transmission medium.
The measure of the bits transferred across the media over a given period of time

questions.question: What is the broadcast address of the network 192.168.100.200 \ 255.255.255.248?
What type of attack is the Ping of Death?
Convert the following decimal number into hexadecimal: 593
How does the service password-encryption command enhance password security on Cisco routers and switches?

options.text: It requires all remote connections to use SSH.
It applies a strong MD5 hash to the enable secret password.
It encrypts passwords that are stored in router or switch running configuration files
It prevents the configuration file from being copied or viewed by unauthorized users.

questions.question: Which IPv4/IPv6 coexistence method is described as 'A method of transporting an IPv6 packet over an IPv4 network. The IPv6 packet is encapsulated inside an IPv4 packet'?
Which IPv4/IPv6 coexistence method is described as 'the devices run both IPv4 & IPv6 protocol stacks simultaneously'?

Which IPv4/IPv6 coexistence method is described as 'Network Address Translation (NAT64) allows IPv6-enabled devices to communicate with IPv4-enabled devices using a translation technique similar to NAT for IPv4'?
Which network technology allows devices to communicate using both IPv4 and IPv6 addressing at the same time?
What is the difference between the terms keyword and argument in the Cisco IOS command structure?

options.text: A keyword is a user-defined parameter, while an argument is a predefined variable.
A keyword is only used in EXEC mode, while an argument is only used in configuration mode.
A keyword is a specific parameter. An argument is not a predefined variable.
A keyword is always a number, while an argument is always a word.

options.text: The PC was pre configured using DHCP
The PC used an APIPA address to access the router.

options.text: It requires all hardware vendors to use the same proprietary protocols.
